title: Perceive, Reason, Act
concept: The core loop of an agent: observing the environment, reasoning about state, and taking actions that change the environment.

exercises.prompt: Welcome to AI Agents! Quick hook: what makes an AI 'agent' different from a simple chatbot that just answers one question?

options.text: An agent can take actions in a loop to reach a goal, not just reply once
An agent is just a chatbot with a nicer font
An agent can never make mistakes

exercises.explain: An agent doesn't just answer and stop. It keeps looping: it looks at the world, thinks about what to do, and takes an action to move toward a goal. That loop is what we'll explore in this unit!

cards.title: Like Playing a Video Game
cards.body: When you play a game, you look at the screen, decide your move, then press a button. Then the screen changes and you do it again. An AI agent works the same way, over and over.

cards.title: The Agent Loop
cards.body: An AI agent runs a repeating loop: Perceive (observe the world), Reason (think about what to do), and Act (do something that changes the world). Then it perceives again.

cards.title: Driving a Car
cards.body: A driver looks at the road (perceive), decides to slow down for a red light (reason), and presses the brake (act). The car stops, the world changes, and they look again.

cards.title: A Robot Vacuum
cards.body: It senses a wall ahead (perceive), decides to turn (reason), and steers left (act). Now it faces a new spot and repeats the whole loop.

cards.title: Not Just One Answer
cards.body: Myth: an agent thinks once and is done. Truth: an agent keeps looping, checking the results of its actions and adjusting until the goal is reached.

cards.title: Chatbot vs Agent
cards.body: A plain chatbot replies and stops. An agent loops through perceive, reason, and act to actually get things done.

exercises.prompt: What are the three steps of the core agent loop, in order?
exercises.explain: The agent first perceives (observes the world), then reasons (decides what to do), then acts (changes the world). After acting, it perceives again and the loop continues.

exercises.prompt: True or False: After an agent acts, the environment can change, so it needs to perceive again.
exercises.explain: Actions change the world. That's why the agent loops back to perceive: it must see the new situation before deciding its next move.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing step of the agent loop.
exercises.sentence: The agent must first Perceive the world before it can Reason about it.
exercises.explain: Before an agent can reason or act, it needs information. That first step of observing the environment is called Perceive.

exercises.prompt: Which description of an AI agent sounds like it was written by an AI trying to sound smart but empty?

options.text: An agent leverages synergistic paradigms to holistically optimize actionable outcomes across dynamic ecosystems.
An agent watches its surroundings, decides what to do, and takes an action.
A robot vacuum senses a wall, turns, and keeps cleaning.

exercises.explain: The first option is stuffed with buzzwords like 'synergistic paradigms' but never explains anything real. Clear, concrete language beats fancy filler.

exercises.prompt: A drone is told to find and land on a red mat. It scans the floor, spots the red mat to its left, and rotates toward it. Match each part of this to the agent loop.

options.text: Scan the floor = Perceive, spot the mat = Reason, rotate = Act
Scan the floor = Act, rotate = Perceive, spot the mat = Reason
All three steps are the same thing
The drone only reasons and never perceives or acts

exercises.explain: Scanning the floor is Perceive (gathering info). Spotting the mat and deciding to turn toward it is Reason (making a choice). Rotating is Act (changing the world). After it moves, it will scan again, repeating the loop until it lands. This perceive-reason-act cycle is the heartbeat of every AI agent.
